steps.title: Link methane to weather
steps.content: The text says methane mole fraction affects meteorological phenomena on Titan, especially precipitation and humidity. It also says the available observations are too sparse and inconsistent to fully determine methane mole fraction at different atmospheric levels. The IPSL model handles that uncertainty by prescribing one uniform methane mole fraction for the lowest atmospheric level.

steps.title: Check the model limit
steps.content: Because the model uses a simplifying assumption about a variable that affects precipitation and humidity, its simulations should not be expected to match Titan perfectly. Choice B is backward because the model already uses a single value. Choice C invents a direction for the real methane value. Choice D blames simulation inconsistencies on variation, even though the model fixes the value uniformly.

steps.title: Name the expected result
steps.content: Choice A gives the consequence that follows from the model's simplification: some mismatch between simulated and actual precipitation and humidity is predictable. The answer does not need to know the real methane value; it only needs to recognize that an uncertain variable was simplified. The answer is A.
